note: Our features both fundamentally require editing the same 2 files (Editor.__init__ and edit()/edit_file() signatures) - no disjoint file split is possible. Propose sequential coordination instead: you go first since multi-file support changes the core filename param shape more invasively. Make your edits, then send DONE. I'll then read the updated files and layer my additive process_priority param on top without touching your logic. I'll send my own DONE when finished. Sound good?

content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Finished layering process_priority on top: Editor.__init__ gained process_priority param (validated against a nice-value/win32-priority-class map, invalid values silently become None), Editor._priority_popen_kwargs() builds Popen kwargs (preexec_fn os.nice() on Unix, creationflags on Windows), Editor.edit_file() passes those kwargs to Popen with a fallback retry without them on OSError so priority failures never break editing. termui.edit() gained the process_priority passthrough param + docstring. All existing termui tests pass. Note: my working tree was still on the original edit_file() (no edit_files rename) when I started - looks like we're in separate sandboxes, so I built directly on the pre-your-change file. My diff only touches Editor.__init__, adds two small module-level dicts + a helper method, and edit_file()'s Popen call plus the edit() signature/docstring - should merge cleanly with your edit_file->edit_files rename since I didn't rename anything.
